_note: Worked example: Harborline Provisions, a fictional regional foodservice distributor. This spec is the primary teaching artifact for the build engine — it exercises shorthand generators, inference, traits, per_parent rows, fk affinity matching, state machines, SQL derivations (real lineage), views, and logged imperfections. Keys starting with '_' are comments and are stripped before validation.

vocab._note: Per-category product pools so names, categories, storage zones, and shelf life stay mutually coherent.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables._note: CRM is the customer system of record. created_at is sorted so account IDs correlate with onboarding date, like a real sequence-keyed table.
tables.name: account
tables.purpose: CRM customer accounts (restaurants, institutions, chains).

columns.name: account_name
columns._note: Chains draw from a small shared brand pool with numbered locations ('Cedar Point Cantina #3'); independents/institutions get unique one-off names.

columns.name: email
columns._note: Role/proprietor addresses on the account's own fictional subdomain (orders@cedar-point-cantina.example.com) — all-personal emails on a B2B account master are a tell.

columns.name: created_at
columns._note: sorted: account IDs correlate with onboarding order; backfill: 65% of accounts predate the data window (tenured book), so in-window volume grows ~7%/yr instead of ramping from zero.

tables._note: ERP customer is generated 1:1 per CRM account (parent_copy keeps attributes coherent across systems); imperfections later make the two systems disagree realistically.
tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: ERP customer master, fed from CRM but maintained separately.

tables.name: product
tables.purpose: Product master with cost and tiered list pricing.

columns.name: product_name
columns._note: category is drawn first, then the name comes from that category's pool — names, zones, and shelf life stay coherent.

tables._note: Exactly 4 warehouses, one per region, so fk affinity matching on region has full coverage.
tables.name: warehouse
tables.purpose: Distribution centers.

tables.name: contract
tables.purpose: Customer pricing contracts (chains and institutions).

tables.name: contract_line
tables.purpose: Contracted price per product; discount off list.

tables._note: Orders flow through a right-censored state machine: orders near the as_of date are legitimately stuck mid-pipeline. Warehouse assignment uses affinity matching on region with a 5% leak.
tables.name: sales_order
tables.purpose: Sales order headers.

rows._note: Volume conditioned on segment (chains order multiples of independents) plus a heavy-tailed per-account activity weight so a whale tier emerges.

columns.name: order_date
columns._note: Calendar-weighted date bounded by the customer's onboarding date: weekday/seasonality shape AND no orders before the customer existed. Use date_offset only for short process lags, never long activity windows (clamping piles rows onto the as_of date).

tables.name: sales_order_event
tables.purpose: Order status history emitted by the state machine.
tables.grain: one row per order per state entered

tables.name: sales_order_line
tables.purpose: Order lines priced from the product list with contract/street mix.
tables.grain: one row per order per line number

columns.name: product_id
columns._note: zipf(0.5): popular SKUs lead but the top item stays ~2-4% of lines, not 20%.

tables.name: inventory_lot
tables.purpose: Received lots with expiry for perishables.

columns.name: expiry_date
columns._note: Shelf life conditioned on category: produce days, frozen months.

tables.name: driver
tables.purpose: Delivery drivers per DC.

tables._note: Routes and stops are DERIVED from shipped orders so delivery dates, warehouses, and order statuses are coherent by construction — never wire delivery events to orders with a random FK.
tables.name: route
tables.purpose: Daily delivery routes per DC, one per warehouse per delivery day.
tables.grain: one row per warehouse per delivery date

tables.name: delivery_stop
tables.purpose: Stops with proof-of-delivery notes, derived from shipped orders.

tables.name: case
tables.purpose: Exception and review queues.

tables.name: case_event
tables.purpose: Case status history.

tables.name: change_event
tables.purpose: Field-level change history on CRM accounts.

columns.name: old_value
columns._note: Old/new values must be plausible for the changed field — random strings here are the first thing a reviewer notices.

tables.name: job_run
tables.purpose: Batch job run log; each run belongs to a catalog job, with occasional failures.

columns.name: error_note
columns._note: Non-null only when the run failed or ran partially.

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (results come from the dq.rule_result_current view).

cases.5: product category missing approved GL mapping

tables._note: Everything below is populated by SQL derivations from the source tables above — real lineage, not parallel-faked data.
tables.name: crm_account_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly CRM extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: crm_account
tables.purpose: Cleaned CRM accounts; normalizes raw extract dates.

tables.name: entity_identifier
tables.purpose: Source-system identifier crosswalk for canonical customers.

tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Canonical customer joining CRM and ERP through the crosswalk.

tables.name: product
tables.purpose: Canonical product.

tables.name: invoice
tables.purpose: Invoices derived from invoiced orders (1:1 with order).

tables.name: invoice_line
tables.purpose: Invoice lines copied from order lines at invoicing.
tables.grain: one row per invoice per line

tables.name: payment
tables.purpose: Payments against paid invoices.

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_customer
tables.purpose: Customer dimension from the canonical layer.

tables.name: dim_product
tables.purpose: Product dimension.

tables.name: fact_order_line
tables.purpose: Order line fact.
tables.grain: one row per sales order line

tables.name: fact_invoice_line
tables.purpose: Invoice line fact.

tables._note: Landing layer (rung 1). One raw.* table per source feed: verbatim text payloads with file/batch ids and ingested_at. format_drift corrupts the date columns post-derivation; the DQ views read these live and catch unparseable dates.
tables.name: erp_order_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly ERP sales-order header extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: erp_invoice_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly ERP invoice extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: wms_inventory_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly WMS inventory-lot extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables._note: Staging layer (rung 2). One stg.* table per landing table: typed, trimmed, case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL), row_number dedup against re-sent batches.
tables.name: erp_order
tables.purpose: Cleaned ERP sales-order headers; parses landed dates.

tables.name: erp_invoice
tables.purpose: Cleaned ERP invoices; parses landed dates.

tables.name: wms_inventory
tables.purpose: Cleaned WMS inventory lots; parses landed dates.

tables._note: Human-entered mapping table. Finance maintains category -> GL revenue line in a spreadsheet, uploaded here. Applied asymmetrically: mart_finance_revenue joins it, mart_salesops_demand_revenue uses raw category. 6 of 7 live categories mapped (disposables only has an unapproved row -> stays UNMAPPED); two retired-category rows; one conflicting beverage re-upload (last upload wins via row_number).
tables.name: gl_product_category_mapping
tables.purpose: Finance-maintained product-category to GL-revenue-line mapping (spreadsheet upload).

tables._note: Job catalog for the code objects below. job_run history references these; procedures/extracts in catalog.code_object name them.
tables.name: job
tables.purpose: Scheduled job catalog (ETL feeds, refresh procedures, outbound extracts).

tables._note: Materialized view (rung 8). SQLite has no native matviews -> a mart table refreshed by an insert-select from bv_revenue_daily. scale_exempt: ~24 months regardless of multiplier. Paired with the rebuild_mv_sales_month_snapshot code object and its job runs.
tables.name: sales_month_snapshot
tables.purpose: Month-grain sales snapshot, materialized from bv_revenue_daily.

tables._note: Code-object catalog (rung: catalog). Self-registers every view from sqlite_master, plus authored target-platform source for the refresh procedure, AR-close procedure, business_days_between function, and the outbound rebate extract.
tables.name: code_object
tables.purpose: Catalog of deployed code objects (views, procedures, functions, extracts) with source text.

tables.name: lineage_edge
tables.purpose: Lineage edges linking code objects to the tables they read and write.

derivations.name: raw_crm_account_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; format_drift imperfection later corrupts a slice of these, which stg parsing then turns into NULLs that DQ-004 catches.
sql: select cast(account_id as text), account_name, segment, sales_region, city, state, status,

derivations.name: raw_erp_order_extract
derivations._note: ERP order header feed landed verbatim as text with US-format dates; raw_order_date_drift corrupts a slice post-derivation (DQ-006 catches it).
sql: select cast(order_id as text), order_number, cast(customer_id as text), cast(crm_account_id as text),
region, cast(warehouse_id as text), channel, status,

derivations.name: raw_wms_inventory_extract
derivations._note: WMS inventory-lot feed landed verbatim; raw_inventory_date_drift corrupts received dates post-derivation (DQ-007).
sql: select cast(lot_id as text), cast(product_id as text), cast(warehouse_id as text), lot_code, lot_status,
cast(qty_received as text), strftime('%m/%d/%Y', received_date), strftime('%m/%d/%Y', expiry_date),

derivations.name: stg_crm_account
sql: select cast(account_id as integer), trim(account_name), lower(segment), sales_region, city, state, lower(status),

derivations.name: stg_erp_order
derivations._note: Typed/trimmed; case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL); row_number dedup against re-sent batches.
sql: select order_id, order_number, customer_id, crm_account_id, sales_region, warehouse_id, channel, order_status, order_date, ingested_at
select cast(order_id as integer) as order_id, trim(order_number) as order_number,
cast(customer_id as integer) as customer_id, cast(crm_account_id as integer) as crm_account_id,
trim(sales_region) as sales_region, cast(warehouse_id as integer) as warehouse_id,
lower(channel) as channel, lower(order_status) as order_status,
row_number() over (partition by cast(order_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: stg_wms_inventory
sql: select lot_id, product_id, warehouse_id, lot_code, lot_status, qty_received, received_date, expiry_date, ingested_at
select cast(lot_id as integer) as lot_id, cast(product_id as integer) as product_id,
cast(warehouse_id as integer) as warehouse_id, trim(lot_code) as lot_code, lower(lot_status) as lot_status,
row_number() over (partition by cast(lot_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: xref_entity_identifier
derivations._note: A crosswalk that maps a single system is not a crosswalk — include both CRM and ERP identifiers.
sql: select row_number() over (order by c.customer_id), 'customer', c.crm_account_id,
'LedgerWorks ERP', 'customer', cast(c.customer_id as text), date(c.created_at), null, 1
select 100000 + row_number() over (order by a.account_id), 'customer', a.account_id,

derivations.name: core_customer
sql: select s.account_id, s.account_name, s.segment, s.sales_region, s.city, s.state, s.status,
(select cast(x.source_record_id as integer) from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.canonical_entity_id = s.account_id and x.source_system = 'LedgerWorks ERP'

derivations.name: core_product
sql: select product_id, sku, product_name, category, storage_zone, list_price, active_flag

derivations.name: erp_invoice
derivations._note: due_date honors each customer's credit terms (COD/NET14/.../NET60) and aged invoices flow through a collections lifecycle instead of sitting 'open' forever.
sql: select o.order_id, o.customer_id, date(o.invoiced_at) as invoice_date, o.status,
date(o.invoiced_at, '+' || case c.credit_terms when 'COD' then 0 when 'NET14' then 14
when 'NET45' then 45 when 'NET60' then 60 else 30 end || ' days') as due_date
from erp.sales_order o join erp.customer c on c.customer_id = o.customer_id
select b.order_id, 'INV-' || printf('%07d', b.order_id), b.customer_id, b.invoice_date, b.due_date,
(select round(sum(l.extended_amount), 2) from erp.sales_order_line l where l.order_id = b.order_id),
when julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(b.due_date) > 120 and b.order_id % 9 = 0 then 'written_off'

derivations.name: erp_invoice_line
sql: select l.order_line_id, i.invoice_id, l.line_number, l.product_id, l.quantity, l.unit_price, l.extended_amount, null
from erp.sales_order_line l join erp.invoice i on i.invoice_id = l.order_id;

derivations.name: raw_erp_invoice_extract
derivations._note: ERP invoice feed landed verbatim from the derived invoice table; runs after erp_invoice so the invoice rows exist.
sql: select cast(invoice_id as text), invoice_number, cast(customer_id as text), invoice_status,
printf('%.2f', invoice_total), strftime('%m/%d/%Y', invoice_date), strftime('%m/%d/%Y', due_date),

derivations.name: stg_erp_invoice
sql: select invoice_id, invoice_number, customer_id, invoice_status, invoice_total, invoice_date, due_date, ingested_at
select cast(invoice_id as integer) as invoice_id, trim(invoice_number) as invoice_number,
cast(customer_id as integer) as customer_id, lower(invoice_status) as invoice_status,
row_number() over (partition by cast(invoice_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: erp_payment
derivations._note: Real AR is imperfect: ~4% pay in two installments, ~3% short-pay by cents-to-dollars. Exact 1:1 full payments for every invoice is a synthetic tell.
sql: select i.invoice_id, i.invoice_total, date(o.paid_at) as pay_date
from erp.invoice i join erp.sales_order o on o.order_id = i.invoice_id
case when invoice_id % 10 < 5 then 'ACH' when invoice_id % 10 < 8 then 'check' else 'card' end,
case when invoice_id % 23 = 0 then 'Partial payment 1 of 2.'
when invoice_id % 31 = 0 then 'Short pay, freight deduction.' else null end
select 'PAY-' || printf('%07d', p.invoice_id) || '-2', p.invoice_id, date(p.pay_date, '+12 days'),
from (select i.invoice_id, i.invoice_total, date(o.paid_at) as pay_date
from erp.invoice i join erp.sales_order o on o.order_id = i.invoice_id
where o.paid_at is not null and i.invoice_id % 23 = 0) p;

derivations.name: tms_route
sql: select distinct o.warehouse_id, date(o.shipped_at, '+1 day') as route_date
where o.shipped_at is not null and date(o.shipped_at, '+1 day') <= '2025-12-31'
row_number() over (partition by warehouse_id order by driver_id) - 1 as rk,
count(*) over (partition by warehouse_id) as cnt
select row_number() over (order by s.warehouse_id, s.route_date),
and d.rk = cast(strftime('%j', s.route_date) as integer) % d.cnt;

derivations.name: tms_delivery_stop
sql: select o.order_id, o.warehouse_id, date(o.shipped_at, '+1 day') as delivery_date
where o.shipped_at is not null and date(o.shipped_at, '+1 day') <= '2025-12-31'
row_number() over (partition by r.route_id order by s.order_id) as stop_sequence
join tms.route r on r.warehouse_id = s.warehouse_id and r.route_date = s.delivery_date
select row_number() over (order by route_id, stop_sequence),
case when order_id % 7 = 0 then 'Signed by receiver.'
when order_id % 7 = 1 then 'Left at loading dock.'
when order_id % 7 = 4 then 'Short one case, credit requested.'

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_customer
sql: select customer_uid, account_name, segment, sales_region, status from core.customer;

derivations.name: wh_dim_product
sql: select product_uid, sku, product_name, category from core.product;

derivations.name: wh_fact_order_line
sql: select l.order_line_id, o.order_id, o.order_date, o.crm_account_id, l.product_id,
o.warehouse_id, o.channel, l.quantity, l.unit_price, l.extended_amount, o.order_status
join stg.erp_order o on o.order_id = l.order_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_invoice_line
sql: select l.invoice_line_id, l.invoice_id, i.invoice_date, cu.crm_account_id, l.product_id,
join stg.erp_invoice i on i.invoice_id = l.invoice_id
join erp.customer cu on cu.customer_id = i.customer_id;

derivations.name: nv_order_line
derivations._note: Normalized view (rung 6): wide re-join of the order-line fact to its dims with business column names. No aggregation. left joins because orphan products / unmastered customers legitimately lose dim rows.
sql: f.warehouse_id, f.channel, f.quantity, f.unit_price, f.extended_amount, f.order_status
left join wh.dim_customer dc on dc.customer_key = f.customer_key
left join wh.dim_product dp on dp.product_key = f.product_key;

derivations.name: nv_invoice_line
sql: left join wh.dim_customer dc on dc.customer_key = f.customer_key
left join wh.dim_product dp on dp.product_key = f.product_key;

derivations.name: nv_invoice
derivations._note: Invoice-header normalization: maps erp customer_id to the crm account key so the AR stack joins dim_customer.
sql: select i.invoice_id, i.invoice_number, cu.crm_account_id as customer_key, dc.account_name, dc.segment,
join erp.customer cu on cu.customer_id = i.customer_id
left join wh.dim_customer dc on dc.customer_key = cu.crm_account_id;

derivations.name: bv_revenue_daily
derivations._note: Business view (rung 7): aggregation + running total via sum() over. Built on nv_order_line.
sql: round(sum(sum(extended_amount)) over (order by order_date rows between unbounded preceding and current row), 2) as revenue_running_total

derivations.name: bv_ar_aging
derivations._note: Business view: aging case ladder over nv_invoice.
sql: select customer_key, account_name, segment, invoice_id, invoice_number, invoice_date, due_date, invoice_total,
cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(due_date) as integer) as days_past_due,
from nv_invoice where invoice_status not in ('paid', 'written_off');

derivations.name: bv_customer_economics
derivations._note: Business view: per-customer revenue with window-ranked whale tiers over nv_order_line.
sql: select customer_key, account_name, segment, orders, revenue,
rank() over (order by revenue desc) as revenue_rank,
ntile(20) over (order by revenue desc) as revenue_ventile,
case when ntile(20) over (order by revenue desc) = 1 then 'whale'
when ntile(20) over (order by revenue desc) <= 4 then 'key'

derivations.name: mv_sales_month_snapshot
derivations._note: Materialized view (rung 8): insert-select from bv_revenue_daily into the mv.sales_month_snapshot table. The rebuild_mv_sales_month_snapshot code object refreshes it on a schedule.

derivations.name: mart_sales_daily
derivations._note: Rewired onto the stack: BU view over the business view bv_revenue_daily.
sql: select order_date, orders, units, revenue, revenue_running_total

derivations.name: mart_ar_aging
derivations._note: Rewired onto the stack: BU view over bv_ar_aging.
sql: select customer_key, invoice_id, invoice_number, invoice_date, due_date, invoice_total, days_past_due, aging_bucket

derivations.name: mart_finance_revenue
derivations._note: Business-unit view (rung 9), FINANCE. Applies the human-entered GL mapping (left join + coalesce to UNMAPPED), dedups the conflicting beverage re-upload to the latest approved row, and EXCLUDES written-off invoices. Competes with mart_salesops_demand_revenue.
sql: row_number() over (partition by source_code order by uploaded_at desc, mapping_id desc) as rn
where approved_flag = 1 and (effective_end_date is null or effective_end_date > '2025-12-31')
left join mapping m on m.source_code = n.category and m.rn = 1

derivations.name: mart_salesops_demand_revenue
derivations._note: Business-unit view, SALES OPS. Raw category codes, NO GL mapping, INCLUDES all invoice statuses (write-offs included). Legitimately disagrees with finance on the grand total by exactly the written-off amount.

derivations.name: control_recon_order_invoice
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW so it reflects post-derivation imperfections (restatement reversals on invoice lines create the breaks).
sql: select ol.order_id, round(ol.total, 2) as order_total, round(il.total, 2) as invoice_total,
from (select order_id, sum(extended_amount) as total from erp.sales_order_line group by order_id) ol
join (select i.invoice_id as order_id, sum(l.extended_amount) as total
from erp.invoice_line l join erp.invoice i on i.invoice_id = l.invoice_id
group by i.invoice_id) il on il.order_id = ol.order_id

derivations.name: control_recon_finance_salesops
derivations._note: Reconciles the competing revenue metric. salesops_gross (all invoice statuses, raw category) minus finance_net (written-off excluded, GL-mapped) must equal the written-off revenue; unexplained_difference proves it. finance_unmapped_revenue quantifies the mapping coverage gap (the disposables UNMAPPED bucket).
sql: with sales as (select round(sum(extended_amount), 2) as gross from nv_invoice_line),
fin as (select round(sum(extended_amount), 2) as net from nv_invoice_line where invoice_status != 'written_off'),
wo as (select coalesce(round(sum(extended_amount), 2), 0) as written_off from nv_invoice_line where invoice_status = 'written_off'),
and not exists (select 1 from manual.gl_product_category_mapping m
from sales s, fin f, wo w, unmapped u;

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'active account missing email' as issue, cast(account_id as text) as entity_id
from crm.account where status = 'active' and (email is null or email = '')
select 'DQ-002', 'order line references unknown product', cast(order_line_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from erp.product p where p.product_id = l.product_id)
select 'DQ-003', 'erp customer missing xref mapping', cast(c.customer_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.source_system = 'LedgerWorks ERP' and x.source_record_id = cast(c.customer_id as text))
from raw.crm_account_extract where source_created_date not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-005', 'product category missing approved GL mapping', c.category
from (select distinct category from core.product) c
where not exists (select 1 from manual.gl_product_category_mapping m
from raw.erp_order_extract where order_date_raw not like '__/__/____'
from raw.wms_inventory_extract where received_date_raw not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-008', 'lot past expiry still available', cast(lot_id as text)
where lot_status = 'available' and expiry_date is not null and expiry_date < '2025-12-31';

derivations.name: catalog_lineage_edge
derivations._note: Lineage edges for the authored procedures/extracts -> the tables they read and write.
sql: insert into catalog.lineage_edge (edge_id, code_object, operation, table_name, edge_note) values

derivations.name: catalog_code_object
derivations._note: Authored target-platform source for the procedures/function/extract, then self-registration of every deployed view from sqlite_master so the catalog provably matches the code. Runs LAST so all views exist. business_days_between is intentionally stale (references a renamed column) — noted as known debt.
sql: insert into catalog.code_object (object_name, object_type, language, source_text, owner, created_at, last_altered_at, notes) values

('rebuild_mv_sales_month_snapshot', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure rebuild_mv_sales_month_snapshot() language sql as $$ delete from mv_sales_month_snapshot; insert into mv_sales_month_snapshot (month_key, active_days, orders, units, revenue) select strftime(''%Y-%m'', order_date), count(*), sum(orders), sum(units), round(sum(revenue),2) from bv_revenue_daily group by 1; $$;', 'data_engineering', '2024-01-15 09:00:00', '2025-02-03 11:20:00', 'Scheduled monthly by integration.job rebuild_mv_sales_month_snapshot.'),

('period_end_ar_close', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure period_end_ar_close(as_of date) language plpgsql as $$ begin insert into workflow_case (case_type, queue, status) select ''credit_hold'', ''credit_desk'', ''open'' from erp_invoice i where i.invoice_status = ''in_collections'' and business_days_between(i.due_date, as_of) > 45; end; $$;', 'finance_controls', '2024-01-20 08:30:00', '2025-01-31 17:45:00', 'Opens collections cases at period end; calls business_days_between.'),

('business_days_between', 'function', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace function business_days_between(d1 date, d2 date) returns integer language sql as $$ select cast(julianday(d2) - julianday(d1) as integer) - 2 * (cast(julianday(d2) - julianday(d1) as integer) / 7); $$;', 'data_engineering', '2023-11-02 10:00:00', '2023-11-02 10:00:00', 'KNOWN DEBT: references retired column invoice.terms_days in an older overload; flagged for cleanup.'),

('nightly_invoice_rebate_extract', 'extract', 'sql', 'select i.invoice_number, i.invoice_date, i.customer_id, round(sum(l.extended_amount),2) as rebate_basis from erp_invoice i join erp_invoice_line l on l.invoice_id = i.invoice_id where i.invoice_status = ''paid'' group by 1,2,3; -- format: CSV; destination: sftp://rebate-processor.example.com/in/; schedule: 0 23 * * *', 'integration_eng', '2024-03-01 09:00:00', '2025-04-10 09:15:00', 'Outbound nightly feed to the rebate processor (job nightly_invoice_rebate_extract).');
insert into catalog.code_object (object_name, object_type, language, source_text, owner, created_at, last_altered_at, notes)
from sqlite_master where type = 'view' and name not like 'sqlite_%';

imperfections.name: crm_duplicate_accounts
imperfections.scenario: Reps create duplicate accounts; MDM review queue exists for this.

imperfections.name: orphan_order_lines
imperfections.scenario: Discontinued products hard-deleted from the product master.

imperfections.name: order_manual_overrides
imperfections.scenario: CSRs override pricing/terms with supervisor approval.

imperfections.name: raw_date_format_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy extract job pads dates differently after a patch.

imperfections.name: missing_customer_xref
imperfections.scenario: Mappings lost during the 2024 integration migration.

imperfections.name: stg_name_conflicts
imperfections.scenario: Staging dedup logic mangles some names vs source.

imperfections.name: invoice_line_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Billing corrections issued as reversal + restated pairs; these create the order-vs-invoice recon breaks.

imperfections.name: duplicate_job_runs
imperfections.scenario: Scheduler retries log duplicate run records.

imperfections.name: raw_order_date_format_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy ERP order extract pads dates differently; DQ-006 catches the unparseable ones.

imperfections.name: raw_inventory_date_format_drift
imperfections.scenario: WMS extract date format drifts after a patch; DQ-007 catches it.

controls.rule_name: Order lines tie to invoice lines
Finance net revenue reconciles to sales-ops gross demand
controls.expected_break_rate: difference equals written-off revenue; unexplained_difference must be 0

dq_rules.condition: every live product category has an approved GL mapping
available lots are not past expiry

dataflows.name: customer_mastering
dataflows.sequence: crm.account -> raw.crm_account_extract -> stg.crm_account -> xref -> core.customer -> wh.dim_customer

dataflows.name: view_stack
dataflows.sequence: wh facts/dims -> nv_* -> bv_* -> mv_sales_month_snapshot / mart_sales_daily / mart_ar_aging / mart_finance_revenue / mart_salesops_demand_revenue

dataflows.name: order_to_cash
dataflows.sequence: erp.sales_order -> state machine -> erp.invoice/invoice_line -> erp.payment -> wh facts -> marts

dataflows.name: code_catalog
dataflows.sequence: sqlite_master views + authored procs/extracts -> catalog.code_object; catalog.lineage_edge; integration.job -> integration.job_run

dataflows.name: gl_mapping
dataflows.sequence: manual.gl_product_category_mapping -> mart_finance_revenue (applied) vs mart_salesops_demand_revenue (not) -> control_recon_finance_salesops + DQ-005 + workflow gl_mapping_request
